column14: site_charac

Road & river to NW; river to E-SE. PIRE/PIST dominate canopy; QURU/ACRU/POTR dominate subcanopy. Ferns/PIST saplings/blueberries/grass on floor. Some CWD, treefall gaps, medium rocks. Revised plot center; other too close to road (<80m) .
Lots of downed trees.  High moss cover.

Canopy dominated by PIRE/PIST; subcanopy contains ABBA/ACsp/FRAM/PRsp. Ground covered by green vine, grasses & some herbs. Open gappy canopy. Bedrock close to surface. River down steep slope to S. Some CWD - mainly small fallen trees.
Yep, cedar bogs do exist! Moss, almost exclusively cedar w/ spruce/fir understory - scattered yellow birch
narrow wet riverbed. FrNi, ThOc, BeAl present in canopy, TsCa in understory and at edges
large gap @ B&E, rainbow @ E, many large boulders, mossy ground, large hemlocks
Gnarly stunted ABBA forest. Low canopy ~25', some BEPA. Lots of throwdowns and rotting logs, rocks, deep moss layer.
Gappy overstorey of ABBA/BEPA; dense ABBA sapling undergrowth, lots of dead/downed logs ~6-8 tk. Ferns
column16: ACRU in shrub/sap layer. Original plot straddles stream."

column14: hemlock dominant, some Ac(sp), BeAl, FaGr; lots of downed trees covered in moss
just slightly upslope from a boggy wetland area, lots of rock covered in mosses
near bog, mostly spruce, lots of moss
valley
large basswood with 5 trunks as centerpoint\n
heavy shrub/sap layer, lots of downed trees
approx. 300 ft. from Lake Eaton

Relatively open FAGR/ACSM stand with some CWD on floor. Moderate-low herb cover. Understorey ACPE/FAGR. A couple large BEAL in canopy. Slopes to lake. Some medium gaps around plot. Canopy FAGR trees in decline due to fungus.
low beech trees, tall maples and BeAls and BePas
Near a water reservoir, spruce in understory surrounded by slopes but main center is on a little plateau
Boggy but dry at center, lots of snags, lots of gaps, glacial outwash area, very mossy mesic, lots of organic matter in soil.
Overlooking a lake, Unk species, poss hornbeam? AcRu in lower slope, QuRu in upper slope. Large PiSt and TsCa dominant, few herbs on slope, more at bottom (C).
Mainly AcSm in canopy. Moved east of original AK36, which was only ~90ft from road.
QuRu dominant in canopy, FaGr dominant in shrub/sap understory. Rocky ground.
Dense canopy, AcSm, BeAl, AbBa, few rocks, some fallen trees, FaGr

NH/maple stand with a few very large FrAm and BeAl trees. Down trees and CWD on forest floor. Lots of leaf debris on ground. Some large standing dead birch leaving gaps in canopy. Dominated by FrAm, BeAl, AcSm. FaGr in the understory.
FaGr, BeAl, big TsCa, AcSm, and AcRu. Two areas of blowdown trees, between a little valley.
Mainly AcRu/BeAl canopy. Dense understory (FaGr), hardwood plot. Occasional PiRu, some AcPe and FaGr in understory.
Very steep, about 200ft below original AK47, same composition.
Maple (AcSm), beech, BeAl. Pretty open. About 10-15m from G Lake.
Pine (PiSt) and AcRu. Gap at subplot A (2 dead pines). About 500ft from G Lake.
Large birch (BePa), fir and spruce. Shallow soil (and loose), rocky, but covered by leaf fall and moss. Lots of shining clubmoss on ground.

Some wood sorrel, bunchberry. Ground cover sphagnum, ferns, and scattered saplings (<0.5m) with pockets of open water. Canopy open and gappy with AbBa, AcRu, BeAl, and PiRu dominant. Lots of CWD - primarily small fallen trees. Lots of lichens and mosses on live trees.

Shining clubmoss, ferns and saplings (FaGr and AcPe mostly) dominate ground layer. AcSm and FaGr dominate the canopy; interspersed by large BeAl. Some large moss-covered rocks and a moderate amount of CWD around site. One treefall gap on transect E. Lake at bottom of slope to gen N direction
High fern cover. Balsam fir, paper birch stand with some CWD on forest floor. Some standing snags, more gappy towards the north.
Some wind damage on
Rocky old high-grade logging; wet from heavy rains. Sub-surface stream/ flow. Maple saplings (4 meters). Minor FTC damage.
Surface fire. Logged? Mesic (wet from heavy rains). Minimal understorey. Some rocks, minimal detritus.
Surface fire, minor; mesic; some rocks and fallen debris (organic)
Distubred woods, plot unevenly bisected by stone wall. Oaks/Ash reaching 20-25m. Some logging, wind damage. Relatively thick understorey. Moderately mesic.
Ground cover washed out from recent flooding. Much CWD. Looks like logged 30-50 years ago.
White pine plantation
Lots of decaying CWD; rocky; xeric area at B, E - otherwise intermediate (A, C, D)
Xeric; near bluff edge; visible gypsy moth damage on white oak leaves; grass understorey
Xeric ridgetop, lots of understorey and saplings; large white pines spread throughout
Wet, near bog/ wetland. Some detritus. Significant understorey. Gappy canopy. Open water near plot/ under
Mesic; lots of down trees near and surrounding plot; some from recent blowdown, others prior; little to no understorey; probable browsing on plot; no seedling layer
Flat, NH stand, previous study plot with decomp bags remaining. Some large down trees with single treefall gaps in canopy, minimal understory with grass dominating.
Upland; heavy grazing; no saplings; some little coarse woody debris; ACSM, QURU and QUAL in the upper canopy; OSVI at one end of the plot
Flat, upland NH/Oak stand, large red and white oaks acround plot; red oak DBH 70cm. very low understory cover with lots of bare ground showing. A few large fallen trees aruond plot.
Recent wind throw/ blowdown in vicinity. Mesic. Old fallen tree boles surrounding and within plot. Limited understorey/ herb layer. No seedlings. QURU in canopy, ACSM codominant.
Oak, NH stand with large red oaks (>76cm) surrounding and within plot; fell just outside of 20x20m plot. Little to no understory with some grass covver. Moderate CWD on forest floor.
Very open; sandy soils; some large-size coarse woody debris; canopy dominated by oak (mostly QURU); understorey is maple with some basswood
Moderate mesic; generally high herb layer; sapling vegetation to south of plot
Plot was flooded during 2010 late summer/early fall flood on WI river
PIST and QURU in the canopy; ACSM in the subcanopy; PIST seedlings on forest floor
Rolling terrain with some grass cover and lots of bare ground. Moderate amount of CWD on forest floor. Some white pine in sub canopy/understory some moss cover.
Plot slopes toward river with hemlock lining edge of island. A few large down trees. Little to no understory with some grasses. A couple large gaps in the canopy with smaller AcSm capturing the gaps.
Upland NH stand with a large amount of CWD on forest floor. Some large tree fall gaps from recent blowdowns, understory of grasses, no saplings or shrubs.
near campsite 15 (NW 30m from center)
subplot B is 10-15 m from a road
Steepslope to C (25%)
lots of ferns & grasses, old logging road through plot, lots of huge red oaks dead, stripe maples make up shrub
stream tributaries (multiple) in plot, heavy rhododendron
stream at edge of plot, plot goes form level valley to uphill slope
lots of ferns, some downed trees
some mortality of QURU (gypsy moth?) & QUPR, some moss & laurel
Thick understorey and herb layer; some single tree-fall gaps; moderate degree of organic debris; PIST and BEPA in the canopy, ACSP and COAM beneath (see drawing)

column13: Ridge/valley, only about 20m long, 5m up and down
column14: Undulating terrain, light windthrow. Heavy canopy competition between PIBA, ABBA and POTR. Some organic debris. Mesic; rocky substrate - rocks less than one foot below litter layer (rocks greater than 6 inches in depth)
Logged. Moderate mesic. Medium sized red pines; some organic debris on forest floor; high herb and understorey layer
Black spruce; lowland, bog; sphagnum moss ground layer; Labrador Tea understorey; some old cutting evidence; minimal cut stumps
Lots of ferns, diverse shrub layer but relatively homogenous overstorey
Wetland; low canopy; mostly black ash canopy
NWC lowland; broken canopy of mostly THOC with seedling layer of THOC, ABBA, FRNI, ALRU. Most canopy trees standing at angle, lots of ground layer texture.
Sphagnum wetland with few to no openings in moss cover, with dense black spruce growth, and few taller tamaracks.

Terrain slopes toward railroad. Previously logged. Many maple seedlings. Some >40cm stumps, CWD and fallen logs. Mesic, deep rich topsoil. High canopy closure. Evidence of surface fire; previously contained wet spp. Some gaps (small, single trees)
Near spruce bog; mesic upland; moderate amounts of fallen debris/detritus; self thinning debris; thick understorey
FRAM and ACSM in canopy
Logging; mesic; dense herb layer; some detritus; open; near slope to stream bottom
Logged (old clear cut); mesic; relatively open canopy; strong ABBA regeneration in sapling layer; lots of understorey gappy canopy layer
Red pine plantation with strong hazelnut understorey
Old clearcut colonized by POTR, which are beginning to self-thin. Heavy amount of coarse woody debris on site.
Tamarack bog with sphagnum and labrador tea understorey; open; hydric; peat bog; some organic detritus
Lowland bog; hydric; sphagnum ground layer; black ash canopy; thick and wet understorey; high moss cover; lots of organic debris on forest floor (fallen logs)
Black spruce, tamarack bog; Labrador Tea and Sphagnum moss on the ground layer; coarse woody debris; LALA comprises the upper canopy; mostly broadleaf understorey
Mesic; some debris on surface; thick understorey below knee height; near small stream; relatively closed canopy
COAM and PRsp understorey; shrubs (some maple seedlings/saplings); evidence of logging; dry mesic (in between); near swamp/bog edge; plot fully covered
Previously logged; maple/ hazelnut understorey; shrub/sap layer; moderate mesic; thick understorey (below maple layer)
Note: samples collected from bottom may more closely resemble mid/topo levels as whole aspen canopy is sunlit
bottom land, hardwood forest w/ some standing water, herb/grass understory. seedlings and saplings of maple and ash. some large down trees and CWD. rock soil.
mesic mixed NH stand with AcSm dominate along w/ basswood and ash in the canopy. high ash regen in seed/saplayer, site was thinned in the past 10-15 year. some CWD on forest floor, grass ground cover
White pine stand
red pine plantation with high CoAm cover in understory w/ a few AcRu, AbBa, & PiGl. High herb/grass cover. Not much CWD on forest floor. Typical RP plantation

Upper convex slope white pine stand, maple (sugar and red) and yellow birch in sub canopy. white pine dominates canopy. some thinning in the distant past (decaying stumps and nurse logs). lots of CWD. mat of white pine needles under herb layer. Some standing dead snags (>35cm)

Plot opposite side of road from WC flux tower. NH forest with maple, ash, cherry, basswood, and oak. Former research plot, tagged trees, old sap flow mounts, litter baskets, etc. Moderate shrub/sap layer, high herb cover. Lots of ferns, high grass cover, undulating terrain, some trenches and low spots with water, slopes east from road. maples and ash beginning to senesce.

NH/Red oak stand, some large exposed rocks, undulating terrain with tipup mounds, lots of CWD on forest floor; high grass cover. Some leaf litter on ground. Some moderate canopy gaps with saplings capturing the gaps. Maples beginning to senesce.
Hemlock/ hardwood stand. Undulating terrain, some CWD. Minimal shrub and sap layer. Lots of dead leaves on ground.
Red pine plantation. High herb layer. Some CWD. Relatively open under canopy.
Hemlock lowland with undulating terrain. Some wet swampy spots with standing water. Large down trees on forest floor. Moderate amounts of other CWD. Single treefall gaps.
Upland, northern hardwood/maple stand. Some large down trees on forest floor and treefall gaps. High herb and moss cover. Small saplings of spruce in understorey.
Aspen hardwood stand. Lots of CWD. Aspen in overstorey. Subcanopy dominated by ACSM; Ash and Balsam Fir also present in the subcanopy.

Undulating terrain with soil mounds and some coarse woody debris. Several larger fallen logs and evidence of some wind throw on stand fringes. Surrounded by thinned forest stands. Little to no understorey in majority of plot.

Sloping terrain; old tip-up mounds. Soil has some texture. Some down trees and cwd on ground. Sparse understorey/ herb layer; some areas have more. ABBA regeneration in shrub/sapling layer. Mesic. Some windthrow evidence. Note: ACSM may be ACRU.
Undulating terrain. ABBA in understorey. Thick understorey/ herb layer. Some down trees and cwd. Evidence of surface fire. Likely and old logged stand.
Moderately mesic; minimal coarse wood debris and fallen trees on forest floor; some evidence of logging (selective cutting?)

Current defoliation; 2-5% mortality/ affected (standing dead, recently fallen). High degree of understorey herbs, high miss cover. Some coarse woody debris on ground (0-1%). Extensive honeysuckle and woodland rush.
Xeric. High moss cover. Insect distribution on oaks (ping pong gall), rust, black flies on underside of leaves. Some red pine regeneration. A few piles of slash.
Moss cover about 95-100%. Bracken fern 5-10% cover. Sedge/gr 50-75%. Blueberry 10-25%. Defoliation mild. Very little coarse woody debris - 5 logs in census area. Lichen 0-2% on all trees.
Xeric to mesic down slope. Previously logged stand, lots of standing dead/ snags. Moderate CWD on forest floor. Thick herb layer, high shrub/sap cover. Some recent JP budworm damage (up to 10%).

Xeric. Fair amount of CWD on forest floor. Significant amount of standing dead PIBA. Some QUMA in understorey layer. Likely logged in the past. Some recent (current) budworm damage (5-15%). Soil ripped with slash rows.
Maybe(?) a plantation. Healthy looking trees; some budworm damage. Very little CWD on forest floor. Thick herb layer, relatively few shrubs. Some standing dead. Some open grown trees, gappy stand.

Jack pine, oak stand. Lots of poison ivy, shrub layer of oak, several standing dead PIBA snags. Some large gaps in canopy, lots of CWD on forest floor. Low herb cover, lots of leaf litter. Some recent down PIBA (wind); lots of mortality.

Ridge tip forest with sloping terrain on either side of plot center. Lots of CWD on forest floor. Some standing snags. Lots of leaf litter on forest floor. Moderate herb layer. Some recent wind distrubance.

column13: Upper convex slope; top of slope
column14: Maybe a plantation. Xeric; mostly grasses in the herb layer. High moss cover. Some large gaps with open grown PIBA. Lots of down trees down slope (windthrow). Several standing dead PIBA likely from competition. Other CWD on ground. Short canopy.
logged red pine plantation, very minimal understory with some grasses and moss cover, dense needle mat on forest floor, some small slash piles, few fallen limbs. 40-50 foot tall canopy
Undulating ground, wetter pockets with microclimates of lowland vegetation/ trees. Large stumps spread throughout plot (second growth stand). Sugar maple dominated. Leeky" plot."
Adjacent to ravine, flat. Hemlock and maple dominated. Large CWD on forest floor, several windthrow blowdown areas. Mostly open, high herb layer. 2km to lakeshore.

Standing water in low pocket. POTR/QURU/ACS/TIAM in canopy. Lots of CWD. Distinct temporary drainage streams down slope - lots of ferns and leeks there. Relatively low sapling layer dominated by ACSM. Furrows and soil mounds present.
South facing oak/ maple stand. Gypsy moth caterpillar found. Outcropping rock on A and E. 1-2 very large tree CWD found on plot. Looks like some of the oaks have been partially defoliated.

Dominated by large hemlocks; lots of CWD; low shrub/sap layer; small dirt mounds upon which the trees grow - small lower areas in between dominated by ferns; lots of Oxalis on forest floor; small dry temporary wetland toward C; one large tree-fall gap; lots of deer scat

Black spruce lowland/ bog (sphagnum bog). High labrador tea and blueberry herb cover. Some small shrubs and saplings. Sogy sphagnum moss ground layer. Canopy is relatively gappy. Some CWD on FF, lots of natural stumpage.
Red pine stand; herbaceous plants include Aralia (nudicaulis?) and Canada mayflower; some ferns, lycopods; COAM main understorey shrub; thin canopy; slopes down to lake (north of plot)
Lowland aspen/ HW stand. COAM the main understorey shrub. Small pockets of dried lowland areas dominated by graminoids and ferns. Canopy thin and sunny. Dry water channel beds.

Some poison sumac. Lowland cedar stand with pockets of standing water. Soft sphagnum moss humps; high moss cover over entire plot. Overstorey is somewhat gappy with cedar, tamarack, black spruce and fir. Cedar dominates overall canopy strata. Some black spruce in overstorey. Plot traversed by LALA near B.

Lots of CWD, small fallen trees. Relatively flat terrain with slight undulations; outcropping large portions of bedrock covered in mosses and lichens; abundant fern growth. Understorey dominated by COAM and ABBA; canopy dominated by PIRE and PIBA. POTR along B edge; C very close to forest road. Mosquito overpopulation of area.
